critique: The delivered result is a finite-dimensional LP formulation whose main sharpness step becomes a direct two-point mean-completion construction once the missing inequalities are added. The advertised law-sharp interval is sharp only for a very permissive completion class with arbitrary off-arm product kernels, so the causal content is much narrower than the continuous-treatment DiD headline suggests. The critical-L theorem is standard parametric LP basis sensitivity, and the inference theorem gives conservative pointwise outer coverage by enlarging the feasible set with diverging padding rather than a sharp or broadly usable inference theory. This is a real but specialized algebraic partial-identification note, not a field-level contribution.
